chain_edges.notes: Vercel-Context.ai OAuth supply-chain pattern feeds the Mythos+Mercor third-party-vendor boundary thesis.
Agent-in-the-loop secret exfiltration risk motivates contractor-credential and naming-entropy hardening at frontier preview vendors.
Tighter third-party preview vendor baselines reinforce the staged gating that keeps frontier weights closed.
Agent Registry convergence (AWS / NemoClaw / Hermes) feeds directly into the broader hyperscaler agent control plane battleground.
OAuth-scope AI-extension carve-out at SSO providers is the same identity-plane move that elevates agent IDs to first-class principals.
Headless-preferred metadata on registry/MCP surfaces is a substrate the control-plane competition standardizes on top of.
Agent-in-the-Loop secret exfiltration risk pressures hyperscalers to bind audit/governance into a unified agent control plane.
Local-first inversion thesis (35B-A3B + Bonsai) sets up the 27B Dense + 1M context standardization claim.
Native 1-bit edge-LLM trend reinforces shift to commodity VRAM defaults that the 27B Dense recipe also targets.
Open 27B Dense flagship pushes proprietary tier to API-only, sharpening the local-vs-frontier split via Qwen Max-Preview.

relations.notes: Both extend the Vercel-Context.ai supply-chain frame: OAuth-scope category vs third-party-vendor boundary baseline.
Independent facets of the agent/AI security stack: contractor + URL entropy vs CI agent secret exfiltration.
Both forecast formalized AI-security categories by Q3 2026; access-control vs prompt-injection CVE category.
Parallel hardening tracks: third-party preview-vendor baseline vs GGUF model-supply-chain signing.
A unified hyperscaler agent control plane structurally requires a registry standard for skills, tool permissions, and audit traces.
Both predict SSO providers carving out new categories by Q3 2026 (agent-ID principals vs AI-extension OAuth scopes); different facets of the same identity shift.
Hyperscaler-frontier-lab exclusives and the agent control-plane race are independent facets of the same H2 2026 hyperscaler-platform consolidation.
Headless-preferred MCP/registry metadata and agent control-plane competition are compatible adjacent platform-shape developments.
Both describe local-inference standardization; 27B Dense is supply side, local-first is adoption side.
Open 20-30B Dense floor and closed-frontier ceiling are complementary halves of the same local/cloud split.
Different recipes (Dense 27B FP16-class vs native 1-bit) for the same edge-deployment outcome space.
